purpose: Decide how backend scope changes should land after carrier-fit audit, without turning one industry or one customer data shape into platform schema.
kernel: platform -> company -> business -> carrier -> fact -> projection

decision_gates.when_to_use: The family has clear carrier fit and current fields already express the business boundary.
required_evidence: family has low or no future platform pressure
no current workflow needs business before carrier
no report requires business aggregation independent of carrier
decision_gates.verification_gate: carrier-fit registry remains complete and model audit has zero unclassified models

decision_gates.when_to_use: A family has medium or high pressure, but current workflows can keep existing project binding while carrying additional business/carrier metadata.
forbidden_changes: using optional metadata as source-of-truth before acceptance probes exist
required_evidence: named pressure model in carrier-fit registry
workflow exists before carrier or may span carriers
old and new read paths can coexist
decision_gates.verification_gate: runtime forms keep existing project behavior and reports can ignore empty optional scope

decision_gates.when_to_use: Management visibility needs business-level or carrier-level aggregation, but fact-side ownership is already sufficient.
allowed_changes: refresh owner and idempotency key update
forbidden_changes: ad hoc aggregation outside registered refresh path
required_evidence: aggregation cannot be expressed by existing company/project projection
refresh owner and acceptance probe are declared
decision_gates.verification_gate: projection registry has no unregistered projection models and refresh probe is declared

decision_gates.when_to_use: Approval, validation, capability, or visibility policy must apply at company/business/carrier scope without owning facts.
allowed_changes: scene or capability contract scope metadata
forbidden_changes: policy requiring project for non-project industries
frontend-specific rendering logic in backend policy
required_evidence: scope can be evaluated without customer-specific data
decision_gates.verification_gate: policy/capability registries remain platform-scoped and do not introduce business fact ownership

decision_gates.when_to_use: A company-managed business must exist before any industry carrier, span multiple carriers, or own lifecycle/reporting independent of carrier.
allowed_changes: compatibility link from existing industry carrier
forbidden_changes: customer replay table promoted as platform business model
required_evidence: at least one pressure model that cannot honestly require carrier
business lifecycle needed before or outside carrier
migration and acceptance probes are defined
decision_gates.verification_gate: schema proposal cites carrier-fit registry, source models, migration plan, and user-visible acceptance probes

decision_gates.when_to_use: A generic carrier registry/table is needed because a business may use multiple industry carriers or the same platform feature must operate across non-project carriers.
forbidden_changes: replacing project.project inside construction workflows without migration
using generic carrier table as a duplicate fact table
required_evidence: more than one carrier type is needed
business-to-carrier relationship is not one-to-one project binding
permission/reporting/scene contracts need generic carrier access
decision_gates.verification_gate: carrier binding registry names each industry carrier and compatibility adapters keep existing forms working

current_application.reason: Tender is business-before-carrier pressure, but current construction workflows still rely on project.project; add non-required scope metadata only after design and probes.
